constitutional_findings: Worker invocation authority exists only at the governed AiGOL invocation boundary.
EXECUTION_READY proves preparation and does not grant execution authority.
Execution authorization must be explicit, bounded, replay-visible, revocable, and non-transferable.
Worker assignment and dispatch are required before invocation.
Conversation, cognition, Resource Selection, PPP, providers, replay, improvement intent, and Workers cannot invoke Workers.
Pre-invocation validation, post-execution result validation, and post-execution replay review are mandatory.
Replay records evidence and never creates, restores, or extends authority.
remaining_gap_before_first_governed_work: A governed execution authorization runtime is not implemented by this milestone.
A governed Worker assignment and dispatch runtime is not implemented by this milestone.
A governed Worker invocation runtime is not implemented by this milestone.
Worker sandbox, effect controls, result validation runtime, and post-execution replay review must be certified before real execution.
